section.title: Overview
section.para: The rust_concurrent_calls module provides per-account concurrent call limiting for &osips;. It tracks active calls per account (typically identified by From-&uri; username) and enforces configurable per-account limits loaded from a CSV file.
Accounts not listed in the limits file are subject to a configurable default limit. An account with a limit of 0 is effectively blocked from making any calls.

section.title: How it works
section.para: The module supports two operating modes:

Automatic mode (default): When dialog.so is loaded and auto_track is enabled, the module automatically increments the counter when a dialog is created (DLGCB_CREATED) and decrements it when the dialog terminates or expires (DLGCB_TERMINATED/DLGCB_EXPIRED). The account is extracted from the SIP message using the configurable account_var pseudo-variable (default: $fU). In this mode, you only need to call check_concurrent() in the script; increment and decrement are handled automatically.

Manual mode: When auto_track is disabled (set to 0) or dialog.so is not loaded, the module uses explicit concurrent_inc() and concurrent_dec() calls from the script. This gives the script author full control over when counts are adjusted.
The manual functions (concurrent_inc() and concurrent_dec()) remain available regardless of the auto_track setting, allowing mixed usage if needed.

Typical usage (automatic mode):
Load dialog.so before this module.
On initial INVITE (no to-tag): call check_concurrent() to verify the account is under its limit. The counter is automatically incremented when the dialog is created.
No need to handle BYE or failure responses for counter management — the dialog callbacks handle decrement automatically.

Typical usage (manual mode):
On initial INVITE (no to-tag): call check_concurrent() to verify the account is under its limit. If allowed, call concurrent_inc() to increment the counter.
On failure responses (3xx-6xx to INVITE): call concurrent_dec() to release the slot.
On BYE: call concurrent_dec() to release the slot.

Per-account limits are loaded from a CSV file at worker startup. Each worker maintains its own independent set of counters, so the effective limit is per-worker. For precise global limiting, consider using an external data store.
The check_concurrent() function sets $var(concurrent_count) and $var(concurrent_limit) pseudo-variables for use in script logging or response messages.
Limits can be reloaded at runtime using concurrent_reload() without restarting &osips;. Active call counters are preserved across reloads; only the per-account limits are updated.

section.title: &osips; Modules
section.para:
The following modules must be loaded before this module:
dialog — Required for automatic tracking mode (auto_track=1). If dialog.so is not loaded and auto_track is enabled, the module falls back to manual mode with a warning.

section.title: External Libraries or Applications
section.para:
The following libraries or applications must be installed before running &osips; with this module loaded:
None.

section.title: limits_file (string)
section.para: Path to a CSV file defining per-account concurrent call limits. Format: one line per account as account,max_calls. Lines starting with # are treated as comments. Blank lines are ignored. This parameter is required. The module will fail to load if this parameter is not set or the file does not exist.

para.emphasis: Default value is None (must be set).

section.title: default_limit (integer)
section.para: Default concurrent call limit applied to accounts not listed in the limits file. Must be at least 0; a value of 0 blocks all unlisted accounts.

section.title: auto_track (integer)
section.para: Enable automatic dialog callback tracking. When set to 1 and dialog.so is loaded, the module automatically increments the concurrent call counter when a dialog is created and decrements it when the dialog terminates or expires. This eliminates the need for explicit concurrent_inc() and concurrent_dec() calls in the script.
When set to 0, the module operates in manual mode where the script must explicitly manage counters.
If auto_track is enabled but dialog.so is not loaded, the module logs a warning and falls back to manual mode.

example.title: Set auto_track parameter
example.programlisting:
... # Enable automatic tracking (default) modparam("rust_concurrent_calls", "auto_track", 1)
# Disable automatic tracking (manual mode) modparam("rust_concurrent_calls", "auto_track", 0) ...

section.title: account_var (string)
section.para: The pseudo-variable expression used to extract the account identifier from the SIP message during automatic dialog tracking. This is evaluated in the DLGCB_CREATED callback.
Common values include $fU (From-URI username), $rU (Request-URI username), or any other PV expression that identifies the account.
This parameter is only used when auto_track is enabled.

example.title: Set account_var parameter
example.programlisting:
... # Use From-URI username (default) modparam("rust_concurrent_calls", "account_var", "$fU")
# Use Request-URI username modparam("rust_concurrent_calls", "account_var", "$rU") ...

section.title: use_dialog_profiles (integer)
section.para: When set to 1, use dialog profiles (via get_profile_size() and set_dlg_profile()) for cross-worker accurate concurrent call counts. This requires dialog.so to be loaded.
When set to 0, the module uses a per-worker HashMap for counting, which is faster but only sees calls handled by the same worker process.

section.title: profile_name (string)
section.para: The dialog profile name used for cross-worker counting when use_dialog_profiles is enabled. This profile must be configured in the dialog module.

section.title: direction_aware (integer)
section.para: When set to 1, enable per-direction (inbound/outbound) limits. The limits file must have three columns: account,max_inbound,max_outbound. The direction-specific functions check_concurrent_inbound() and check_concurrent_outbound() become available.
When set to 0 (default), the module uses a single limit per account with the two-column format account,max_calls.

section.title: cooldown_secs (integer)
section.para: Cooldown period in seconds after a limit rejection. When an account hits its concurrent call limit, all subsequent calls from the same account are rejected for this duration without checking the counter.
When set to 0 (default), no cooldown is applied. Each call is checked against the counter individually.

example.title: Set cooldown_secs parameter
example.programlisting: ... # Reject all calls for 30 seconds after a limit hit modparam("rust_concurrent_calls", "cooldown_secs", 30) ...

section.title: burst_threshold (integer)
section.para: Burst detection threshold. When an account's concurrent call count increases by this many within burst_window_secs, flag it as a burst. Set to 0 to disable burst detection.

section.title: burst_window_secs (integer)
section.para: Time window in seconds for burst detection. The module tracks concurrent call count snapshots within this window and compares the current count to the oldest snapshot.

section.title: publish_events (integer)
section.para: Enable event publishing. When set to 1, the module publishes significant events as NOTICE-level structured log messages that operators can consume via syslog or log aggregation. If the NATS module is loaded, events are also published to NATS subjects under opensips.events.*.

Events published by this module: E_CONCURRENT_LIMIT (when a call is rejected at limit) and E_CONCURRENT_BURST (when burst detection triggers). Each event includes a JSON payload with relevant details.

Check whether the given account is under its concurrent call limit. On return, the pseudo-variables $var(concurrent_count) and $var(concurrent_limit) are set to the current count and the effective limit for the account.

This function does NOT increment the counter. In automatic mode (auto_track=1), the counter is incremented by the dialog callback. In manual mode, use concurrent_inc() separately after a successful check.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier to check. Typically $fU (From-&uri; username) or $rU (Request-&uri; username). This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: -1 (denied) - account is at or over its limit (blocked).
-2 (error) - internal error (missing parameter or uninitialized worker).

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: check_concurrent usage (automatic mode)

section.para: Increment the concurrent call counter for the given account. In manual mode, call this after a successful check_concurrent() when the INVITE is being forwarded. In automatic mode, this is handled by the dialog callback, but the function remains available for mixed usage.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier. Must match the value used in check_concurrent(). This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: -2 (error) - internal error (missing parameter or uninitialized worker).

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.
Decrement the concurrent call counter for the given account. The counter floors at zero and will never go negative.

In manual mode, call this when a call ends (BYE) or when an INVITE receives a failure response and the counter was previously incremented. In automatic mode, this is handled by the dialog callback, but the function remains available for mixed usage.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier. Must match the value used in concurrent_inc(). This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: -2 (error) - internal error (missing parameter or uninitialized worker).

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

Re-read the limits CSV file from disk and atomically swap the per-account limits. Active call counters are preserved; only the limit values are updated. This allows changing account limits without restarting &osips;.

The reload affects only the worker process that executes the function. To reload all workers, trigger this function from a route that is processed by each worker (e.g., via an MI-triggered OPTIONS ping or timer route).
On success, the accounts and reloads statistics are updated.
Return codes:

listitem.para: -2 (error) - file read error or worker not initialized.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: concurrent_reload usage

section.para: Retrieve module statistics as a JSON string. Sets $var(concurrent_stats) with a JSON object containing all tracked counters for the current worker process.
Return codes:
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

Check if the given account is experiencing a burst of concurrent calls. Returns 1 if a burst is detected (the count increased by at least burst_threshold within burst_window_secs), or -1 if normal. Sets $var(burst_delta) with the rate of change (delta) in concurrent calls.
Requires burst_threshold to be set to a value greater than 0. If burst detection is disabled, always returns -1.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier to check. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: check_burst usage

section.para: Retrieve detailed status for a specific account as a JSON string. Sets $var(concurrent_status) with a JSON object containing the account name, current count, effective limit, and per-direction breakdown.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier to query. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.
Live override of an account's concurrent call limit. The override persists until the next concurrent_reload() or process restart. This allows dynamic limit adjustment without editing the limits file.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier. This parameter is mandatory.
limit (string) - The new limit value as a string (will be parsed as unsigned integer). This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: concurrent_set_limit usage

section.para: Check whether the given account is under its inbound concurrent call limit. Requires direction_aware=1. Sets $var(concurrent_count) and $var(concurrent_limit) to the inbound count and limit.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: check_concurrent_inbound usage

section.para: Check whether the given account is under its outbound concurrent call limit. Requires direction_aware=1. Sets $var(concurrent_count) and $var(concurrent_limit) to the outbound count and limit.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: check_concurrent_outbound usage

section.title: $var(concurrent_count)
section.para: The current number of active calls for the checked account. Set as an integer by check_concurrent().

section.title: $var(concurrent_limit)
section.para: The effective concurrent call limit for the checked account. Set as an integer by check_concurrent(). This is either the per-account limit from the limits file or the default_limit if the account is not listed.

section.title: $var(burst_delta)
section.para: The rate of change in concurrent calls for the checked account within the burst detection window. Set as an integer by check_burst(). Positive values indicate increasing calls, negative values indicate decreasing calls.

section.title: $var(concurrent_status)
section.para: JSON string containing detailed status for a specific account. Set by concurrent_status(). Includes account name, current count, effective limit, and per-direction breakdown (inbound/outbound).

section.title: $var(concurrent_stats)
section.para: JSON string containing the module statistics for the current worker process. Set by concurrent_stats().
Total number of concurrent call checks performed.
Number of checks where the account was under its limit.
Number of checks where the account was at or over its limit.
Total number of counter increments (concurrent_inc() calls and automatic dialog-created events).
Total number of counter decrements (concurrent_dec() calls and automatic dialog-terminated/expired events).
Number of accounts loaded from the limits file. Updated on reload.
Number of successful hot reloads performed via concurrent_reload().
Number of dialogs automatically tracked via dialog callbacks (only incremented when auto_track is enabled).
Number of burst detections triggered (only incremented when burst_threshold is greater than 0).
Number of calls rejected due to an active cooldown period (only incremented when cooldown_secs is greater than 0).
